title: Reinforcement Learning
html:
ML paradigm where an agent learns a policy by taking actions and receiving reward signals. In Yahir's OSU CS coursework (June 2025), he benchmarked Q learning vs SARSA vs Monte Carlo on what looks like a gridworld / blackjack-style env, sweeping ε for the exploration / exploitation tradeoff. Sits inside the broader spring-2025 capstone block alongside reverse engineering and malware analysis.

TL;DR

  • 10 inbound citations from the June 5 → June 9 2025 window plus an April 21 anchor.
  • Algorithms compared: Q-learning, SARSA, Monte Carlo. Standard MDP-control trio.
  • ε-sweep for exploration/exploitation visible in the screenshot output — empirical comparison, not just theory.
  • Pairs with Monte Carlo method (the underlying sampling approach for one of the three algorithms).
  • Same-semester continuity with reverse engineering (May 2025) and malware analysis lab (June 2025) — Yahir was building both security and ML competence in parallel.

Key facts

  • June 5 — algorithm comparison day. 2025 06 05 screenshot 2025 06 05 at 1 00 42 pm a89bf8f0 / 2025 06 05 screenshot 2025 06 05 at 1 00 48 pm 8dca73cd / 2025 06 05 screenshot 2025 06 05 at 1 00 59 pm d5819d5e — three back-to-back captures of Q-learning vs SARSA vs Monte Carlo plots.
  • June 9 — final analysis. 2025 06 09 screenshot 2025 06 09 at 7 08 00 pm c3996405 — likely the writeup.
  • April 21 — early intro. 2025 04 21 screenshot 2025 04 21 at 8 05 48 pm cf299483 is the earliest RL-tagged screenshot.
  • ε-greedy exploration. Standard policy mechanism, swept across values to show convergence behavior.
  • Gridworld / blackjack env. Classic teaching environments — implies the course is following standard Sutton & Barto textbook structure.

Source summaries

  • 2025 04 21 screenshot 2025 04 21 at 8 05 48 pm cf299483 — earliest

Plus 4 cross-links from reverse engineering, monte carlo method, q learning, sarsa concept pages.

  • reverse engineering · malware analysis lab — same-semester capstone work
